displayTitle: The Joint That Hides Its Own Strength

beats.narration: Bonded structures trade rivets for adhesive, spreading load across the whole surface. Once cured, the joint is hidden — and nothing directly measures its strength.

payload.title: THE HIDDEN JOINT
payload.sub: Adhesive spreads the load, then cures out of sight.

beats.narration: A bond is built in steps — etch and prime, lay epoxy film, cure under heat and pressure. Each step can plant a flaw.

payload.heading: A bond is built — so are its flaws

beats.narration: Disbond: the interface separates. Void: a pocket inside the adhesive. Kissing bond: surfaces touch, sound crosses — but the grip is gone.

payload.heading: Disbond, void, kissing bond
payload.note: AIR GAPS REFLECT · KISSING BONDS PASS SOUND

beats.narration: Defects cluster where process control is weakest: doubler edges, ramps and chamfers, core splices, repair perimeters. Give those zones slow passes.
So remember the limit: ultrasound finds the gap, not the grip. Strength was set in the shop — your scan finds only separations.

payload.sub: Strength comes from process control, not the screen.
